pr.title: Initial sysdolphin structs and matching of id.c and object.c
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Introduced the first sysdolphin C/header groundwork for class/object/ID allocation data, matched the small ID and object initialization routines in C, and propagated newly identified symbol names through assembly callsites. The PR added src/sysdolphin to the build, removed the now-matched object.s, split remaining ID assembly to id_asm.s, and renamed func_8037AD48 to HSD_ObjAllocInit and func_80381C18 to hsdInitClassInfo across many asm files.
what_changed: Added sysdolphin headers defining core HSD class/object/allocation types: HSD_Class, HSD_ClassInfoHead, HSD_ClassInfo, HSD_FreeList, HSD_MemoryEntry, HSD_ObjAllocLink, HSD_ObjAllocData, HSD_Obj, HSD_ObjInfo, IDEntry, and HSD_IDTable.
Added object.c with ObjInfoInit, calling hsdInitClassInfo with extern class info/string symbols and sizeof(HSD_ObjInfo) / sizeof(HSD_Obj), replacing the deleted asm/object.s implementation.
Added id.c with HSD_IDGetAllocData, HSD_IDInitAllocData, and HSD_IDSetup; the remaining ID assembly was renamed from asm/id.s to asm/id_asm.s after removing the matched functions.
Added hash.h with hash(s) defined as s % 0x65, aligning with the 101-entry HSD_IDTable.
Updated Makefile to compile src/sysdolphin/*.c and include src/sysdolphin/ in compiler include paths.
Updated ldscript.lcf to include id_asm.o after id.o so the matched C object and remaining asm preserve link order.
Renamed func_8037AD48 to HSD_ObjAllocInit in asm/objalloc.s, include/functions.h, and many assembly callsites.
Renamed func_80381C18 to hsdInitClassInfo in asm/code_8037E708.s and many class-initialization callsites across sysdolphin-related asm.
smart_moves: Matched small, high-leverage sysdolphin functions first while adding the structural headers needed by many future sysdolphin objects.
Kept unmatched ID code in a renamed id_asm.s and added it to the linker script, allowing partial C migration without disrupting section ordering.
Used #pragma force_active around HSD_IDGetAllocData because it was unused until loaded into a GetAllocData function table; the source comment explicitly notes this was required to match.
Captured an odd but matching behavior in HSD_IDSetup with memset(&lbl_804C23EC, 0, 0x194) and a source comment that the partial memset does not quite make sense but matches.
Propagated meaningful names for central helper routines across existing assembly, improving searchability and making later object/class init decompilation easier.

Defined object/class macros in headers, such as HSD_CLASS_INFO, HSD_CLASS_METHOD, HSD_PARENT_INFO, HSD_OBJECT_INFO, HSD_OBJECT_METHOD, and HSD_OBJECT_PARENT_INFO, to encode access patterns for future C ports.
decomp_lessons: For broad systems like sysdolphin, establishing central structs can unlock many later matches even if only a few functions are converted in the PR.
Partial decompilation can be kept link-safe by splitting a file into matched C plus a renamed asm remainder and explicitly preserving object order in ldscript.lcf.
Do not simplify suspicious constants or memset sizes when they are required for matching; document the oddity in the C source instead.
Unused functions that the original binary still contains may need force_active pragmas until their indirect table references or call graph are fully represented.
Renaming a central helper in assembly is useful decomp metadata: HSD_ObjAllocInit and hsdInitClassInfo clarified many callsites before all callers were converted to C.

Expose provisional externs for still-unnamed data symbols when matching small functions, but keep comments tying them to likely semantic names, such as alloc_data, default_table, hsdObj, hsdClass, and string literals.
naming_conventions: HSD object allocation API uses HSD_ObjAllocInit and HSD_ObjAllocData naming.
Class initialization helper was named hsdInitClassInfo with lowercase hsd prefix, matching the PR's assembly rename.
Object info initializer was named ObjInfoInit rather than HSD_ObjInfoInit in this slice.
ID functions use HSD_ID* names: HSD_IDGetAllocData, HSD_IDInitAllocData, and HSD_IDSetup.
Struct tags use underscore-prefixed names, such as struct _HSD_ClassInfo and struct _IDEntry, with typedef aliases like HSD_ClassInfo and IDEntry.
Header guards in this early slice use lowercase underscore style such as _class_h_, _objalloc_h_, _object_h_, _id_h_, and _hash_h_.
Still-unknown data symbols remained as lbl_ addresses with explanatory comments rather than invented definitive names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted asm/object.s after replacing func_8037E6C4 with ObjInfoInit in C.
Renamed asm/id.s to asm/id_asm.s and removed the matched ID functions from it, leaving remaining unmatched assembly intact.
Added id_asm.o to ldscript.lcf immediately after id.o to maintain expected text ordering.
Used sizeof(IDEntry), sizeof(HSD_ObjInfo), and sizeof(HSD_Obj) in C to reproduce constants observed in assembly, depending on newly defined struct layouts.
Used #pragma push / #pragma force_active on / #pragma pop for HSD_IDGetAllocData because it was currently unused but required in the binary.
Retained exact memset size 0x194 for HSD_IDSetup rather than replacing it with an inferred full-table size expression.
Renamed assembly branch labels/calls from func_8037AD48 to HSD_ObjAllocInit and from func_80381C18 to hsdInitClassInfo without changing code bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine HSD_ClassInfo or HSD_Obj struct layouts introduced in PR16.
Find later decomp work that removes lbl_804C23C0 or lbl_804C23EC extern names from id.c.
Find later PRs that replace hsdInitClassInfo assembly with C or update its prototype.
Find later PRs that convert objalloc.s and fully define HSD_ObjAllocInit in C.
Find later changes to ObjInfoInit naming or HSD object/class initialization macros.
